may take longer in certain tasks, they often outperform their 
sighted peers in others, particularly in reading speed when 
using screen readers. His success exemplifies the capacity of 
blind legal professionals to adapt and excel in highly technical 
fields through the strategic use of assistive technology.
(vii) Yetnebersh Nigussie, an Ethiopian lawyer and disability rights 
activist, attributes her ability to pursue a legal career to the loss 
of her eyesight at the age of five, an event she considers to 
have spared her from early child marriage. She has dedicated 
herself to using education as a tool to empower persons 
with disabilities, particularly women and girls, who often face
